skill.name: vercel-deploy-claimable
skill.description: Deploy applications and websites to Vercel. Use this skill when the user requests deployment actions such as 'Deploy my app', 'Deploy this to production', 'Create a preview deployment', 'Deploy and...
skill.summary: Instantly deploy any project to Vercel with automatic framework detection and claimable transfer URLs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives from pattern-matching on markdown documentation text. The skill is a legitimate DevOps tool that executes bash scripts and makes network calls to Vercel API, which is expected behavior for a deployment skill. No malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 23 instances of 'backtick execution' but these are all markdown code formatting in documentation, not actual shell execution. The skill legitimately invokes bash scripts for deployment.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - Network URLs
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 8 hardcoded URLs but these are documentation examples showing expected output format or user configuration instructions, not actual network request endpoints.

content.value_statement: Deploying applications to production hosting is complex and time-consuming. This skill packages your project and deploys it to Vercel with a single command, providing instant preview URLs.
actual_capabilities: Package projects into deployment-ready tarballs excluding node_modules and .git
Auto-detect frameworks from package.json including Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, and more
Create preview deployments with live URLs
Generate claim URLs for transferring deployments to Vercel accounts
Handle static HTML projects by renaming files for proper routing
Output deployment results in both human-readable and JSON formats
limitations: Requires network access to Vercel API endpoints
May require users to configure network capabilities in Claude.ai settings
Does not authenticate with Vercel account directly
Framework detection relies on package.json presence and contents

use_cases.title: Quick Preview Deployments
use_cases.description: Developers can instantly create preview deployments of their projects to share with team members or clients before merging to production.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers working on React, Vue, or Next.js projects

use_cases.title: Static Site Hosting
use_cases.description: Users can deploy static HTML websites to Vercel without needing to set up a Vercel account or configure deployment pipelines.
use_cases.target_user: Designers and content creators with static websites

use_cases.title: CI/CD Integration Testing
use_cases.description: Teams can use this skill to test deployment configurations and verify builds before integrating with formal CI/CD pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy Current Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my current project to Vercel and give me the preview URL.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - User wants to quickly deploy their working directory

prompt_templates.title: Deploy Specific Directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy the project at /path/to/my-app to Vercel.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - User has multiple projects and wants to deploy a specific one

prompt_templates.title: Deploy and Share Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my Next.js app and format the output with both the preview URL and claim URL for my team.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - User needs deployment results in a shareable format

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Deployment Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: My Vercel deployment failed with a network error. Help me configure my Claude.ai network settings and retry the deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - User encounters network restrictions and needs assistance resolving them

output_examples.input: Deploy my React app to Vercel
output_examples.output: Deployment successful! Preview URL: https://skill-deploy-abc123.vercel.app. Claim URL: https://vercel.com/claim-deployment?code=xyz. Your Next.js app has been deployed. View it at the Preview URL or transfer it to your Vercel account using the Claim URL.
Deployment successful! Detected framework: Astro. Preview URL: https://skill-deploy-def456.vercel.app. Claim URL: https://vercel.com/claim-deployment?code=abc. Your static portfolio is now live.

best_practices: Review the preview URL before sharing with clients or deploying to production
Use the claim URL to transfer ownership of deployments to your Vercel account for long-term hosting
Ensure network capabilities are enabled in Claude.ai settings before deploying
anti_patterns: Do not deploy projects containing sensitive data like API keys or credentials
Do not rely on preview URLs for production traffic without claiming the deployment
Do not deploy large projects without checking Vercel's size limits first

faq.question: Do I need a Vercel account to use this skill?
faq.answer: No account is required for preview deployments. You receive a preview URL to view your site and a claim URL to transfer the deployment to your Vercel account if desired.

faq.question: What frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The skill auto-detects popular frameworks including Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, SvelteKit, Astro, Solid, Angular, Express, Fastify, NestJS, Vite, and many others from your package.json.

faq.question: Why is network access required?
faq.answer: The deployment script must communicate with Vercel's API to upload your project and create deployments. Enable *.vercel.com in your Claude.ai network settings.

faq.question: How do I claim my deployment?
faq.answer: Visit the claim URL provided in the deployment output. This transfers the deployment to your Vercel account where you can manage it like any other project.

faq.question: What files are included in the deployment?
faq.answer: All project files are included except node_modules and .git directories. The tarball excludes these to reduce upload size since Vercel handles dependency installation.

faq.question: Can I deploy static HTML sites without package.json?
faq.answer: Yes. For static HTML projects, the skill packages all files directly. If you have a single HTML file not named index.html, it will be renamed to ensure proper routing.
